body.title: [sprint] Escalation ladder: exhaust the family in-tier (cost-ordered), THEN advance to the architect
body.description:
**Problem (refinement of the shipped escalation driver, T-router-failure-bump-escalation):** the current driver bumps DIFFICULTY one rung on the FIRST capability failure. With the real model slate that skips the bigger same-family model. Akien's resolved ladder for a stuck CODING task: (1) Qwen3-Coder 30B local â†’ (2) Qwen3-Coder 480B cloud (SAME family, bigger, cost-ordered next) â†’ (3) DeepSeek (architect/reasoner) as the cross-family FINAL rung â€” even though Qwen-480B and DeepSeek are roughly 'on a level' (both cloud flagships, not a strict difficulty increase). So escalation = 'exhaust the family AT this tier (cheapestâ†’bigger), THEN advance to the architect', NOT 'bump difficulty every capability failure'. Keeps model FAMILY consistent as long as possible (prompt/tool-use transfer), architect only as last resort. See memory project_hex_ollama_model_slate.

**Proposed shape:** give the driver (or the selector) a TRIED-SET keyed by (source,model). On a CAPABILITY failure: re-select the next-cheapest-UNTRIED capable candidate AT THE CURRENT difficulty (walks Qwen-30Bâ†’Qwen-480B within coding). Only when the current tier's candidates are EXHAUSTED, advance to the architect rung (bump difficulty, or an explicit architect-tier for the domain â†’ DeepSeek). Terminal = architect rung also exhausted â†’ system_alarm + halt (unchanged). This MERGES the difficulty-bump with a tried-set exclusion â€” shares the tried-set mechanism with T-inference-hard-error-fallthrough (which excludes a hard-errored candidate); build the tried-set once, both consume it. The AVAILABILITY path (source-down â†’ None) stays unchanged (re-select same difficulty, no ladder advance).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py (_run_inference walk: tried-set + exhaust-tier-before-advance), unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (route() must accept an exclude-set OR expose ranked candidates so the driver can pick next-untried), possibly routing_buckets.py (architect as a rung distinct from pure difficulty). **Design rules:** ALL inference through the Proxy; route by {domain, task_class}; capability bumps, availability does NOT ('Hex-DOWN is not a branch'); log every escalation hop + the tried-set at the crossing. **Scope boundary:** IN: the family-then-architect ladder (tried-set + exhaust-tier-before-advance) for the capability path. OUT: registry population with the real Qwen/DeepSeek specs (separate, gated on Hex/Ollama-Cloud live); the availability path (shipped); hard-error intra-dispatch fall-through (T-inference-hard-error-fallthrough â€” shares the tried-set). **Test plan (stub-first red->green, model-agnostic â€” uses stub sources at code+design difficulty):** (1) capability failure with two untried code candidates re-selects the second (bigger same-family) BEFORE bumping difficulty; (2) only when both code candidates are tried does it advance to the design/architect rung; (3) a candidate is never retried (tried-set honored); (4) availability failure still re-selects same difficulty without consuming the ladder; (5) architect rung exhausted â†’ system_alarm + halt, no loop. **Completion criteria:** a stuck task walks cheapestâ†’bigger within the family at a tier, advances to the architect only when the tier is exhausted, never retries a tried candidate, and still halts cleanly; tests green. VALIDATION of the real Qwenâ†’DeepSeek behavior is a live run once the models are registered.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] â›” NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. â€” applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. â€” applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. â€” applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). â€” applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory â€” one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact â€” â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy â€” â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven â€” no discriminator. A ticket that â€” applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
â€¦and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) â€” query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that a stuck task escalates through its OWN model family first (consistent prompting/tool-use), reaching the architect only when the family is exhausted â€” so escalation gains capability without gratuitous behavioral shifts.
